name: William Bialek
notes: Theoretical biophysicist, professor at Princeton University (Physics) and CUNY Graduate Center. Pioneer in applying information theory to neural coding and biological systems. Co-advised Dario Amodei (Anthropic CEO) on PhD dissertation "Network-Scale Electrophysiology: Measuring and Understanding the Collective Behavior of Neural Circuits." Co-authored PNAS paper on thermodynamics and criticality

name: Lindsey Raymond
notes: Economist and AI researcher. Postdoctoral fellow in Economics and Computation at Microsoft Research. Will join MIT as Assistant Professor (joint Economics and Computer Science) in summer 2026. PhD from MIT (2024), BA from Yale. Former staffer at White House Council of Economic Advisers (2021-2022). Key research: 'Generative AI at Work' (with Brynjolfsson and Li), studying 5,179 customer support ag

name: Matt Salganik
notes: Matthew J. Salganik is the Alexander Stewart 1886 Professor of Sociology at Princeton University. Leading figure in computational social science — co-founded the Summer Institute in Computational Social Science (SICSS) with Chris Bail in 2017, now a global annual program training researchers in data science methods for social research. Author of 'Bit by Bit: Social Research in the Digital Age' (20

name: Meg Sintzel
notes: Director at the AI Safety Foundation (AISF). Former Managing Director of Marketing and Communications at Accenture Canada, where she worked for 36 years before stepping down in 2025. Previously held roles at Toronto Region Board of Trade and National Public Relations. Brings corporate communications and technology industry expertise to AISF board governance.

name: Bill Morris
notes: Director at the AI Safety Foundation (AISF). Former CEO of Accenture Canada with over 35 years at the firm, holding leadership roles across North America and globally. Serves on several corporate and non-profit boards and is active in the business technology space. Brings executive and governance experience to AISF.

name: John Bargh
notes: James Rowland Angell Professor of Psychology at Yale University. Ph.D. in Social Psychology from University of Michigan (1981). Leading researcher on automaticity, unconscious processing, and priming effects on behavior. Directs the ACME (Automaticity in Cognition, Motivation, and Evaluation) Lab at Yale. name: Annie Stephenson
notes: Researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University). PhD in applied physics from Harvard University. name: Andrea Miotti

Lead author of "A Narrow Path," a comprehensive policy plan proposing a 20-year ban on superintelligence development, an IAEA-like international AI authority, and prohibition of dangerous AI capabilities such as automated AI research. Research spans computational photography, 3D reconstruction, and text-to-3D generation.

name: Trevor Darrell

name: Allison Duettmann
notes: President and CEO of Foresight Institute, a San Francisco nonprofit advancing beneficial frontier technologies. Co-initiated Foresight's AI Safety Grant Program. Focuses on existential risk reduction, AI governance, and longtermist coordination. Co-authored 'Gaming the Future' and 'Superintelligence: Coordination & Strategy'.

(2012), which builds Bayesian models for complex physical domains, particularly seismology. name: Allen Newell
